cpu is a hardware component included on the SOM-TL3588 core board, referred to as the central processing unit (CPU), which is located at the center of the core board. It is manufactured by Rockchip with the model number RK3588-J. The CPU serves as the central processing unit of the core board, providing a dedicated interface for connecting to an eMMC chip and receiving the system clock source from the crystal oscillator Y1. It contains bootROM and is responsible for initiating the boot process. The CPU is a critical hardware component discussed in section 1.1, and its main peripheral resources and performance parameters are described in the text. It is a product mentioned in the text referring to the central processing unit of the system.

rom is a hardware component included on the SOM-TL3588 core board, referring to the read-only memory, which is a type of non-volatile memory used for storing firmware or software. It is described as a hardware resource in the SOM-TL3588 industrial core board, used for permanently written data that is not easily modified, as outlined in section 1.2.

ram is a hardware component included on the som tl3588 core board, referring to the random-access memory hardware component, which is discussed in detail within section 1.3. It is included as a hardware resource in the som-tl3588 industrial core board.

led is a hardware component included on the SOM-TL3588 core board, referring to the light-emitting diode components used as status indicators or functional elements on the SOM-TL3588 industrial core board, as described in section 1.6. The emmc is connected to the processor via a dedicated interface, allowing it to serve as a storage and data transfer solution. It is also mentioned in various documents as a subject for improving its longevity and is sometimes associated with micro sd cards.

It is used in various applications with different clock speed configurations and is mentioned in hardware documentation as part of the hardware design guide. The rk3588j is an industrial-grade CPU model compatible with the core board, operating within a specific temperature range, while the rk3588 is a wide-temperature-grade CPU model also compatible with the core board. The processor is described in hardware documents, including updates to its wide temperature operating range, and its functional block diagram illustrates its internal architecture and module details. The rk3588 is also noted for its use in PCIe30 applications with specific data line and control signal connection requirements.

waken is a signal used in pcie2 0 interfaces that must be connected to a functional pin and cannot be replaced by gpio
sata usb30 cannot be used when pcie2 0 functionality is active  as their corresponding modules are not available

refclkp refclkn must meet the same source clock requirements for pcie30 phy and slot peripherals
